content:
- Executed bug task `logging-archive-ingest-silent-failure-and-unsafe-cleanup`: fixed Postgres ingest worker to stop silently swallowing failures and made log-dir cleanup safe-by-default.
- `src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs`: refactored `step_once` to collect per-stage errors (stage/kind/path tags) while continuing other phases; `run` now emits internal error logs (origin=postgres_ingest) with rate limiting + suppression count, and emits a recovery info message after consecutive failures.
- `cleanup_log_dir` now protects known active signals (`postgres.json`, `postgres.stderr.log`, `postgres.stdout.log`), protects recently-modified files via new config `logging.postgres.cleanup.protect_recent_seconds`, treats metadata failures conservatively + observable, and reports remove failures.
- Hardened config validation in `src/config/parser.rs`: require absolute paths for key logging inputs/sinks, lexically normalize paths before ownership comparisons (prevents ./../ bypass), and validate `archive_command_log_file` not inside `log_dir` independent of file sink.
- Fixed `src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs` JSON escaping bug (quote escaping produced invalid JSON) so wrapper tests pass; also refactored wrapper script writer to avoid clippy too-many-args.
- Updated HA e2e harness DCS init payload JSON to include required `backup.bootstrap` field and cleanup protect field so runtime config decode validation passes.
- Docs updated: configuration + observability + troubleshooting now describe ingest/cleanup invariants and internal error signals.
- Verification: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`, `make docs-build`, `make docs-hygiene` all passed (rerun after clippy fixes).
- Next: proceed to Choose-Task flow (task_switch executed; current_task.txt removed by script).

- Ran Choose-Task flow: scanned `.ralph/current_tasks.md` for any `<passes>false</passes>` / `<passing>false</passing>`.
- Confirmed there are NO failing bug tasks; all bugs listed are passing.
- Found multiple non-passing (not-started) non-bug tasks; selected the next most urgent/strategic one.
- Wrote `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/03-task-high-prio-remove-shell-archive-wrapper-and-current-wiring.md` to `.ralph/current_task.txt` (clears slate by removing generated shell archive wrapper + wiring before reintroducing Rust-native mechanism).
- Next: start that task; it likely touches `src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs`, `src/postgres_managed.rs`, `src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs`, config/docs; then re-run `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`.

- Read `src/config/parser.rs` and located all `archive_command_log_file` validation points.
- Identified parser changes required after wrapper removal: remove hard requirements on path when backup enabled/bootstrap enabled, drop log_dir/tail-path invariants around archive log file, and remove archive-specific test.
- No edits were made yet; next step would be updating parser validations/tests when field is removed from wrapper semantics.

- Inspected `src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs` for wrapper coupling.
- Confirmed ingest currently tails optional `logging.postgres.archive_command_log_file` and emits it as `LogProducer::PostgresArchive` with parser-derived `backup.*` attrs.
- Confirmed runtime tests in this file both build wrapper commands and assert ingestion of archive_command JSONL `backup.event_kind=archive_push`, so wrappers are deeply coupled here.
- Next: remove archive tail path plumbing and adapt tests to no longer depend on wrapper JSONL or `PostgresArchive` producer.

- read current task pointer and task description for wrapper removal.
- inspected src/logging/mod.rs and searched archive_wrapper references.
- identified module export line and runtime callsites that must be updated when deleting wrapper.
- should do next: update postgres_managed.rs/postgres_ingest.rs callsites and remove wrapper tests/docs/config dependencies.

- Searched repository for wrapper generation and restore command wiring.
- Confirmed `postgres_managed.rs` injects both `archive_command` and `restore_command` from one generated wrapper path via `ensure_pgbackrest_wal_wrapper`.
- Confirmed wrapper script in `archive_wrapper.rs` dispatches on positional arg: `archive-push` or `archive-get`, so it handles both archive and restore-like WAL fetch flow.
- Confirmed tests in `postgres_ingest.rs` and `postgres_managed.rs` explicitly create and use the wrapper for backup tests.

- Searched Rust codebase for shell-script generation and command paths while answering query.
- Found only runtime-generated shell script path is `pgtuskmaster-pgbackrest-wal-wrapper.sh` via `src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs`, with associated fake pgbackrest script `fake_pgbackrest.sh` in the same file tests.
- Confirmed no other production runtime module writes executable shell scripts besides wrapper-specific logic; `src/logging/mod.rs` exports `archive_wrapper`, and only production call sites in `src/postgres_managed.rs`/`src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs` reference it.
- Identified harness-only script generation (`src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs` writes a fake `kill` script and spawns `/bin/sh`) and a process-script abstraction in `src/ha/worker.rs` named `ScriptedProcess`, which is not shell-script management.

- Read test suite for archive-wrapper generation/execution assertions with rg/nl over src and tests.
- Found wrapper-specific test coverage in src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs and one integration assertion path in src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs; postgres_managed tests do not assert wrapper command strings, only set backup-related config.
- Next: replace/delete those wrapper-specific assertions and keep equivalent coverage for post-remove archive mechanism (or absence of wrapper side effects).

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and the task file `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/03-task-high-prio-remove-shell-archive-wrapper-and-current-wiring.md`.
- Spawned 24 `explore_spark` subagents to inventory all code/docs/tests/config surface area tied to the generated shell WAL archive/restore wrapper.
- Added a detailed step-by-step execution plan to the task file, with explicit design decisions (fully delete wrapper + remove `logging.postgres.archive_command_log_file` from schema, remove `PostgresArchive` ingest path, remove managed injection of `archive_command`/`restore_command`, update docs/runbooks).
- Marked the plan with `TO BE VERIFIED` per the workflow and stopped before executing any code changes.
- Next: do DEEP SKEPTICAL verification of the plan (must change at least one item), then switch marker to `NOW EXECUTE` before implementation.

- Ran repo-wide `rg -n "archive_command_log_file"` across src/docs/src/.agents/.ralph/tasks/examples and collected line-accurate usages.
- Confirmed remaining references still span runtime code, config schema/defaults/parser, tests/fixtures, and operator docs.
- Noted several references outside the current removal task file (e.g., dcs/state, api/worker, api/fallback, debug_api/worker, runtime/node, ha/worker, worker_contract_tests, ha_e2e startup fixture, examples/debug_ui).
- Identified likely plan additions: explicit list of every config-constructor/test/fixture touchpoint, doc debt in legacy/related task files (stories 02/other bug/docs) that still describe required `archive_command_log_file`, and a clean-up pass to keep repository free of wrapper-era references after field removal.

- Ran docs impact verification for terms: `archive wrapper`, `pgbackrest-wal-wrapper`, `archive_command_log_file`, `archive_command`.
- Search results were restricted to `docs/src` and found all wrapper-log references in operator documentation.
- Hit files: `docs/src/operator/observability.md`, `docs/src/operator/configuration.md`, `docs/src/operator/troubleshooting.md`, `docs/src/operator/recovery-bootstrap-runbook.md`.
- Also confirmed there are no literal docs hits for `pgbackrest-wal-wrapper` or exact phrase `archive wrapper`.
- Next step: user asked only for impact list; no file edits were made in this pass.

- Inspected `src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs` around archive ingestion and related tests.
- Located archive tailing path through `archive_command_log_file`, `PostgresIngestWorkerState`, `step_once`, cleanup protection, and cleanup basenames.
- Confirmed parser dependency on wrapper JSON fields in `normalize_postgres_json` via `pgtuskmaster.backup.*` extraction and tests that assert `backup.event_kind == "archive_push"` for archive producer records.
- Identified `postgres_ingest` test that creates wrapper via `crate::logging::archive_wrapper::ensure_pgbackrest_wal_wrapper` and sets `archive_command` to `"{wrapper} archive-push %p"`.
- Also verified explicit wrapper JSON contract in `src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs`: output includes keys `message`, `error_severity`, and nested `pgtuskmaster.backup` fields.
- Next: decide whether to keep generic JSON parser path and only drop wrapper-specific assumptions vs removing wrapper log ingestion entirely.

- Searched tests/ and src/test_harness for exact strings `pgtuskmaster-pgbackrest-wal-wrapper.sh` and `ensure_pgbackrest_wal_wrapper`; no occurrences in either tree.
- Added broader impact scan for related backup-wrapper terms (`archive_command_log_file`, `archive_command`, restore/pgbackrest wrapper patterns) in those same trees.
- Found config/fixture references that include `archive_command_log_file` in:
  - tests/bdd_api_http.rs (inline LoggingConfig::PostgresLoggingConfig construction).
  - src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs (DCS init JSON payload + runtime `logging` config construction).
- Found only generic shell-process helper names containing `wrapper` in `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs` (sleep wrapper utility), not pgbackrest-specific wrapper wiring.
- Next if asked: remove or migrate the above `archive_command_log_file` fixture fields to anticipate compile/test breakage after wrapper removal work.

- Ran full symbol sweep for `PostgresArchive`, `LogProducer::PostgresArchive`, and `archive_command_log_file`.
- Verified production usage is mostly isolated to:
  - logging model + emit path: `src/logging/mod.rs`, `src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs`
  - managed postgres config injection: `src/postgres_managed.rs`
  - wrapper module+tests: `src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs`
  - config schema/defaults/parser + validation/tests: `src/config/schema.rs`, `src/config/defaults.rs`, `src/config/parser.rs`, plus fixtures in many test/worker files
  - docs/observability/troubleshooting/runbook guidance and generated docs/book artifacts.
- No edits were made; only verification and analysis per request.
- Next step: apply removal/refactor in this order: config/schema+defaults/parser → logging ingest variant/paths → managed config + wrapper callsites/tests → docs/tests cleanup; then run the full make gates (no backward compatibility).

- inspected src/postgres_managed.rs focus on archive_command/restore_command injection and takeover flow
- cross-checked startup orchestration in src/runtime/node.rs for restore/bootstrap sequencing and takeover usage
- cross-checked wrapper generation/escaping in src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs and command tokenization in src/process/worker.rs
- cross-checked validation contracts in src/config/parser.rs and operator docs/tests that reference backup/bootstrap archive/restore behavior
- identified no code edits made; primary impact notes: config side effects in materialize/takeover and test/docs touchpoints, plus one likely risk around path quoting already mitigated by pg_ctl option token escaping

- Verified `archive_command_log_file` across schema/defaults/parser and docs.
- Found field in `src/config/schema.rs` (`PostgresLoggingConfig`, `PartialPostgresLoggingConfig`) as `archive_command_log_file` with no serde rename (snake_case key).
- Confirmed default is `None` in `src/config/defaults.rs` and parser enforces presence/validity in backup/bootstrap enabled flows with error path `logging.postgres.archive_command_log_file`.
- Confirmed parser invariant checks and path checks use the same logical dotted config key.
- Identified source docs in `docs/src/operator/{configuration,observability,recovery-bootstrap-runbook,troubleshooting}.md` and generated artifacts in `docs/book` (`operator/{configuration,observability,troubleshooting}.html`, `print.html`, `searchindex.json/js`).
- No extra serde alias/rename for this key; TOML key remains `archive_command_log_file` under `[logging.postgres]`.

- Re-read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/03-task-high-prio-remove-shell-archive-wrapper-and-current-wiring.md`.
- Read the task file and found the plan ended with `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Did a deep skeptical verification pass using 20+ `explore_spark` subagents focused on code/config/docs/tests surfaces for `archive_command_log_file`, `PostgresArchive`, wrapper generation, and managed-postgres wiring.
- Key plan gaps found: many more config-constructor touchpoints set `archive_command_log_file: None` across `src/api/*`, `src/dcs/*`, `src/ha/*`, `src/runtime/*`, `src/debug_api/*`, `src/worker_contract_tests.rs`, plus generated docs in `docs/book/*` and stale task docs in `.ralph/tasks/*`.
- Updated the plan in the task file to cover those touchpoints, added explicit docs/book regeneration + docs make targets, adjusted `rg` sweeps to avoid `.ralph/progress/*` noise, and changed the marker from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: execute the plan exactly as written (no more exploration), deleting the wrapper module + wiring, removing `archive_command_log_file`/`PostgresArchive` ingest, updating all affected config literals/fixtures/docs (including `docs/book/*`), then run `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`, `make docs-build`, `make docs-hygiene`.
